Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

realization in Krsna consciousness

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2008-06-11 · 2 answers
How will you define realization in Krsna consciousness? And when you have one (hopefully), how are you sure that it is true.
user [38] · 2008-06-12
One could give many examples but I like this one: If you look at a ceiling in the dark using a narrow beam of flashlight, youll see only a small part of the ceiling. This is like a material vision. Spiritual vision means broadening the beam and seeing a greater part of the ceiling. Broadening the vision, seeing more and more subtle connections, realizing ones insignificant position and how Krishna is in control.
user [2] · 2008-06-12
the example of the person being in a street with a narrow view and another flying over the city to get a better perspective.
to realize is to "be aware", conscious, and ultimately Krishna conscious
